danger_signals.visual_form: Goblins, ghosts, magical walking cauldrons, spiders, leprechauns, bats, diving turkeys, barracudas
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate threat to Porky; collision causes health loss

danger_signals.visual_form: Spooky Sid (Haunted Woods), Six Shootin' Sheriff Sam (Dry Gulch), Willie Great White (Atlantis), Hyde Tweety (Mines), Monster Max (Alps), Herman the robot (Castle)
danger_signals.meaning: Level-end threat requiring defeat to progress

danger_signals.visual_form: Caged hooligans, creatures with rifle barrels, raining cats and dogs and fish skeletons
danger_signals.meaning: Passive world dangers that damage on contact

danger_signals.visual_form: Daffy Duck in vampire costume appearing on certain platforms
danger_signals.meaning: Startling threat that propels Porky upward (mechanic threat converted to utility)

danger_signals.visual_form: HUD hearts decreasing when hit by enemies
danger_signals.meaning: Direct damage indicator; loss of all hearts = life lost

safety_signals.visual_form: Red X marks placed throughout levels
safety_signals.meaning: Safe respawn point; Porky plants flag to resume from this location on life loss

safety_signals.visual_form: Fruit bowl, heart items, cupcakes, cake, potion (inflatable state)
safety_signals.meaning: Health recovery, temporary invincibility via inflation, life extension, score boost

safety_signals.visual_form: Solid ground, chains to swing on, doorways to enter, underwater bubbles to ride
safety_signals.meaning: Safe traversal zones and progression pathways

safety_signals.visual_form: World map showing progress between levels
safety_signals.meaning: Safe navigation between level zones; visual indicator of advancement

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Checkpoints marked with red X flags that Porky plants

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Horror-themed creatures (goblins, ghosts, cauldrons, spiders, bats, western outlaws, sea creatures, robots, counts)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Theme-specific variants: Western (moose-trophy creatures, Sheriff Sam), Underwater (diving turkeys, barracudas), Castle (Herman robot, Daffy the Count Duck), Alpine (cat/dog/skeleton rain)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Enemies spawn within thematic level environments

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Daffy The Count Duck; vampire-costumed Daffy Duck
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Vampire costume variant vs. standard appearance
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Appears on specific platforms; final boss in castle level

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Surreal, horror-themed environments; Looney Tunes characters as grotesque/scary versions; distorted landscapes (raining cats/dogs/skeletons)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies actively hunt Porky; supernatural mechanics (floating via potion, bubble riding, chain swinging)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Forest setting with season-specific palette; CMS Global 1 controls tree appearance and swimming pool palette; unused creatures (frog, cuckoo bird) suggest dynamic environmental detail
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Goblins, ghosts, cauldrons, spiders, leprechauns, bats spawn; Spooky Sid boss emerges

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Western town environment; tumbleweed decoration; caged hooligans; creatures with moose trophies and rifle barrels
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Six Shootin' Sheriff Sam boss encounter; Western-styled threats

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Underwater ruins aesthetic; CMS Global 1 set to $03 enables Halloween theme variant; Atlantis billboard
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Diving turkeys, barracudas as enemies; Willie Great White boss; bubble-riding mechanics

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Mine setting; CMS Global 1 $01 enables rain in intro; CMS Global 8 $01 replaces lamps with light bulbs; dark cavern aesthetic
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Hyde Tweety boss; TNT boxes present

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Snowy Alps Level (transitioning to Delusional Landscape)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Alpine snow environment transitioning to surreal landscape; weather effects with rain, fog intensity (CMS Global 7 ranges $01-$03); CMS Global 8 enables snow; raining cats, dogs, fish skeletons
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Monster Max boss; dream-logic environmental shifts

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Castle of Daffy The Count Duck (Final Level)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Gothic castle interior; formal throne room for Herman the robot chamber
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Herman robot as final boss; Daffy the Count Duck as level thematic authority

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: CMS Global 2 enables unseen graphics when $01; CMS Global 3 affects background decorations; CMS Global 7 controls rain/fog density; weather randomizes each playthrough (rain, snow, clear)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Visual atmosphere changes affect mood/tension without mechanical impact

environmental_storytelling.example: Each level represents a different nightmare archetype (forest horror, western peril, underwater ruin, abandoned mine, alpine wilderness, gothic castle), visually communicating the nightmare's nature through setting aesthetics

Each level's boss embodies the thematic threat: Spooky Sid = haunted woods spirit, Sheriff Sam = western outlaw law, Willie Great White = oceanic predator, Daffy The Count Duck = gothic nobility. Visual design reinforces their domain authority

Unused graphics (frog jumping into pond, cuckoo bird from window, falling bird, TNT-laden creature) suggest developers conceived rich environmental storytelling through background activity, communicating world-life beyond immediate threats
First level randomizes between Spring, Autumn, Winter, Christmas variants via CMS system, suggesting nightmare recurrence across seasons and holiday disruption themes

Journey escalates from natural horror (forest creatures) through historical/cultural fears (western, ancient civilization, industrial mine, mountain) to pure dream-logic (raining fauna, robotic final guardian, vampire count)
Caged hooligans, rifle-bearing creatures, and raining cats/dogs/skeletons communicate that Porky's nightmare is populated by hostile, absurd, and increasingly irrational threats

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Can move, jump, swing on chains, climb, enter doorways, ride bubbles, collect power-ups
Propelled upward involuntarily; used as puzzle mechanic to reach high platforms
Floats, movable in all directions (up/down/left/right); temporary invincibility period
Spawn in thematic level areas; move toward or near Porky; collision damages player
Appears on specific platforms when stepped on; scares Porky upward; non-lethal interaction converted to mechanic
Unique per level; require defeat to progress; telegraph their thematic domain through appearance and attack patterns
Scattered in levels; collection grants temporary or permanent benefits (health, ammo, lives, floating)
Porky plants flag; marks safe respawn location; visual confirmation of progression and safety establishment

notes: Porky Pig's Haunted Holiday communicates world-state primarily through thematic environmental design and dynamic visual systems (CMS globals). The game lacks traditional faction conflict—instead, all non-Porky entities are nightmare manifestations with no political dimension. Danger is communicated through visual grotesqueness and enemy presence; safety through checkpoints, power-ups, and environmental structure. The innovative random environment system (seasonal variants, weather effects, optional graphics) suggests the developers conceived the nightmare as procedurally variable, adding replayability through visual iteration rather than mechanical change. The game's narrative—traversing nightmares to wake—uses environmental surrealism to escalate from natural to increasingly absurd threats, with the final boss (Daffy the Count Duck) representing the apex of the nightmare's irrational authority. Unused assets indicate deeper environmental storytelling was planned but cut.
